title: Full Installation, Setup, Runtime, Smoke, Logging, Uninstall, And Purge Validation Tasklist
mainGoal: Execute one updated end-to-end validation matrix for Ehecoatl that covers fresh installation, existing-checkout setup, runtime boot, tenant scaffold behavior, request smoke paths, logging and report generation, controlled shutdown, uninstall, explicit purge, rerun safety, and evidence capture for one matching lifecycle performance report.

templateStartTenant.loopbackSmokeMode: Use `https://127.0.0.1` with `curl -sk`, send `Host: fakedomain.com` and `Host: www.fakedomain.com`, and record both cold-path and warm-path behavior.

tasklistPolicy.orderingRule: Run from environment preparation through install, smoke, logging validation, shutdown, uninstall, purge, and rerun checks so every later phase is grounded in the state created by the previous phase.
tasklistPolicy.focusRule: Use the real operator-facing scripts and runtime entrypoints. Prefer decisive pass or fail checks, explicit timing capture, and durable artifacts over ad hoc manual confidence.
tasklistPolicy.readerNote: This tasklist is broader than the earlier lifecycle reruns. It keeps the installer and smoke core, but extends coverage to setup idempotency, runtime observability, hourly logs, tenant `src/report.json`, firewall lifecycle, preservation boundaries, purge correctness, and one rerun after cleanup.

sourceReports.topic: Latest successful install, smoke, uninstall, and clear lifecycle rerun
Latest failed lifecycle rerun showing smoke regression shape
Published operator-facing setup, uninstall, and purge contract
Minimum smoke contract for boot, routing, traffic, and shutdown
Logging, reporting, queue, and runtime configuration behavior

taskGroups.title: Prepare Disposable Execution Target And Measurement Boundary
taskGroups.goal: Make the lifecycle run reproducible, safe to execute, and easy to compare historically.
pointsOfAttention: The full install-to-uninstall cycle should run in a disposable machine, VM, container, or disposable clone so uninstall does not destroy the authoring checkout.
Keep a second checkout available for `setup/purge-data.sh` and report publication after uninstall removes the installed project tree.
Record exact environment details before the run so regressions can be compared meaningfully.

subtasks.title: Use a disposable install target or cloned repository for the real install flow
Keep one separate checkout available for purge and final report writing after uninstall
Verify the target does not contain an active prior `/opt/ehecoatl` install unless the scenario explicitly requires residue handling
Record OS, kernel, Node.js, npm, Redis, PM2, and whether package caches are warm or cold
Record whether `sudo` is non-interactive and whether `iptables`, `acl`, and `systemctl` are available
Record current PM2 list state and existence of `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`

Capture absolute timestamps for install start, setup completion, runtime start, first successful smoke response, stop start, stop complete, uninstall start, uninstall complete, purge start, purge complete, and rerun start
Collect wall-clock durations for each lifecycle phase and notable subphases when visible
Plan one new lifecycle performance report that includes positive results, negative results, and unresolved gaps

taskGroups.title: Fresh Installer Flow Validation
taskGroups.goal: Validate the true fresh-install story and scaffold the default tenant through the published installer path.
pointsOfAttention: This group validates `installer.sh`, not only direct setup.
Use `fakedomain.com` at the first-tenant prompt so setup scaffolds `www.fakedomain.com`.

subtasks.title: Execute `./installer.sh` from the disposable launcher shape
Enter `fakedomain.com` at the tenant prompt and verify `www.fakedomain.com` scaffold creation
Verify `/opt/ehecoatl`, `/usr/local/bin/ehecoatl`, runtime users, runtime directories, and install metadata are created
Measure total installer wall-clock time
Note any manual intervention, retries, dependency repair, or privilege failure
Record whether the flow exits cleanly when re-run against an existing install without `--force`

taskGroups.title: Existing Checkout Setup Flow Validation
taskGroups.goal: Validate the direct `setup/setup.sh` path, including idempotent reentry and explicit `--force` behavior.

tasks.title: Run setup on an existing checkout

subtasks.title: Execute `./setup/setup.sh` on a clean checkout with required privileges
Verify runtime users, CLI symlink, systemd unit, install metadata, and runtime directories are created correctly
Verify the first-tenant scaffold path still creates `www.fakedomain.com` when prompted
Re-run `./setup/setup.sh` without `--force` and verify it exits cleanly without destructive changes
Run `./setup/setup.sh --force` and verify intended reapplication completes successfully
Record timing differences between the first setup and the reentry flows

taskGroups.title: Runtime Boot, Process, And Supervisor Validation
taskGroups.goal: Prove the runtime boots through the published start path and that supervision behaves correctly under normal startup and stop conditions.

tasks.title: Boot runtime through the published operator path

subtasks.title: Execute `cd /opt/ehecoatl/app && sudo npm run start`
Verify the main runtime starts without immediate fatal error
Verify manager and at least one engine process reach ready state
Verify active process labels for main runtime, manager, engine, and any spawned tenant app
Record child count visibility through PM2 logs and any available process-count reporting
Verify no crash loop begins during normal idle runtime
Execute `cd /opt/ehecoatl/app && sudo npm run stop`
Verify connection refusal after stop and verify PM2 no longer serves traffic
Verify stop does not leave orphan child processes, stale PM2 app state, or hanging teardown

taskGroups.title: Tenant Scaffold, Routing, And Smoke Matrix
taskGroups.goal: Validate the scaffold tenant through both default host labels and cover static, redirect, controller, cached, and session routes with cold and warm measurements.

tasks.title: Verify scaffold filesystem and config layout

subtasks.title: Verify host tree contains `src/config.json`, `src/app`, `src/public`, `cache`, `log`, and `spool`
Verify template routes exist for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, `/session`, and `/post-data`
Verify `src/config.json` is readable by the manager according to runtime policy expectations

tasks.title: Run base smoke matrix through loopback

subtasks.title: Issue one strict local request for each route with `Host: fakedomain.com`
Repeat the same matrix with `Host: www.fakedomain.com`
Record status, total time, content type, and whether body shape matches expectations
Verify `/` returns a redirect to `/htm/index.htm`
Verify `/htm/index.htm` returns the hello-world HTML page
Verify `/htm/cached.htm` returns the cacheable HTML example
Verify `/hello` returns JSON with `message` and `timestampUtc`
Verify `/session` returns JSON with `message`, `sessionId`, and `timestampUtc` and sets expected cookies
Verify `POST /post-data` echoes parsed request data correctly for JSON and form bodies
Record first-hit timing for `/hello` and note tenant spawn cost
Capture repeated warm timings for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session`
Reuse a cookie jar for warm `/session` timing so session flow is measured realistically
Request a missing asset path and verify safe `404` behavior
Request a missing controller path and verify tenant-intended `404` or configured failure path
Send an invalid method or unsupported content type and verify deterministic rejection
Send an invalid CSRF request for a session-capable route and verify deterministic rejection

taskGroups.title: Logging, Reporting, And Observability Validation
taskGroups.goal: Verify the runtime now produces the expected operational artifacts and that those artifacts remain meaningful under success and failure.

tasks.title: Validate hourly runtime and error logs

subtasks.title: Generate successful traffic and verify hourly runtime log files are created under the configured base directory
Generate at least one controlled failure and verify the failure is visible in the appropriate error or warning logs
Verify log partitioning by date and hour and verify max-file retention cleanup behavior when practical
Verify `src/report.json` is created under the active host after request traffic
Verify `windowStartedAt`, `lastUpdatedAt`, request counts, status classes, and latency profiles are updated
Verify the report path stays under `src/` even if configuration attempts to escape it
Verify logs capture request completion with latency class and profile
Verify tenant spawn, ready, and stop or reload events are visible enough for operator diagnosis
Record whether child-process counts are easily observable in runtime output or require follow-up tooling

taskGroups.title: Cache, Session, And Queue Behavior Sampling
taskGroups.goal: Exercise the updated runtime paths that most affect correctness under repeated traffic.

subtasks.title: Hit `/htm/cached.htm` repeatedly and verify warm-path stability
Send `If-Modified-Since` for a cached artifact and verify `304 Not Modified` plus `Last-Modified` when appropriate
Verify cache artifact files are created under the host `cache/` folder and stale pointers are handled safely
Verify a new session is created, persisted, and reused across repeated `/session` requests
Verify session writes happen before response writing by checking for any client-visible success paired with session persistence failure
Verify sliding TTL behavior on repeated successful reads when practical to observe
Run a small concurrent burst against static routes and controller routes separately
Verify static and controller traffic use different queue classes by observing behavior or logs
Verify saturated or over-waiting requests return the configured overload status and `Retry-After` guidance

taskGroups.title: Security, Firewall, And Policy Surface Checks
taskGroups.goal: Confirm the main operational guardrails are active during the lifecycle run.

subtasks.title: Verify `firewall_setup` and `firewall_release` are the commands selected by runtime policy defaults
Verify selected non-engine process families receive setup and cleanup calls during start and stop
Record whether firewall setup failures block launch or degrade with warnings according to config
Verify host ownership and mode shape for domain folder, host folder, and `src/config.json`
Verify manager and engine effective access matches runtime policy expectations
Record any ACL or permission repair needed during setup or runtime

taskGroups.title: Uninstall, Preservation Boundary, And Purge Validation
taskGroups.goal: Validate removal and cleanup flows independently so operator expectations remain explicit and safe.

subtasks.title: Execute `setup/uninstall.sh` from the installed tree
Verify `/opt/ehecoatl`, CLI symlink, and install metadata are removed
Verify preserved data under `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` remains present immediately after uninstall
Execute `setup/purge-data.sh` from the spare checkout and confirm with `PURGE`
Verify `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` are removed after purge
Verify no PM2 residue or lingering service state remains after purge

taskGroups.title: Rerun And Regression Safety After Cleanup
taskGroups.goal: Confirm the system can return to a clean rerunnable state after the full removal cycle.

tasks.title: Run a second install or setup after purge

subtasks.title: Repeat either the installer or setup flow after full cleanup
Verify the second run does not depend on hidden state from the first run
Compare first-run and rerun timings and note any large drift
Create one new `report/performance` JSON artifact for the full validation run
Include environment, commands, timings, pass or fail outcomes, positive findings, negative findings, and comparison notes
Call out any skipped scenarios explicitly so coverage boundaries stay honest

finalArtifacts: One new lifecycle performance report under `report/performance/`
Captured logs and command outputs for install, runtime, smoke, stop, uninstall, and purge
One explicit pass or fail table for smoke and observability scenarios
One summary of positives, negatives, and remaining gaps
